name: Pinwheels
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine 1 1/2 cups of all-purpose flour, yeast, sugar, and salt; mix well.
2. Heat water and shortening until very warm (120°F to 130°F). The butter should not melt.
3. Add the warm liquid mixture to the flour mixture.
4. Add eggs. Blend on low speed until moistened.
5. Beat for 3 minutes at medium speed.
6. By hand, gradually stir in the remaining flour to form a soft dough. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 6 to 12 hours. Punch down several times while the dough is chilling.
7. Allow the dough to come to room temperature. Divide into 2 parts.
8. Roll each part into a 10 1/2 x 14-inch rectangle.
9. Cut into 3 1/2 inch squares.
10. Place on greased baking sheets.
11. Cut each square diagonally from each corner, stopping about 1/2 inch from the center. You will have two points at each corner.
12. Lightly brush every other point with egg wash (mix 1 egg and 1 tablespoon water).
13. Bring the moistened points toward the center, gently twist, and pinch to fasten together.
14. Top each center with a small piece of dough, about 1/2 inch in diameter, or use a candied cherry for decoration.
15. Lightly cover; let rise at room temperature until almost doubled (10 to 15 minutes for Quick-Rise Yeast, 15 to 20 minutes for Active Dry Yeast).
16. Bake for 15 minutes at 350°F or until golden brown. Remove to a wire rack and let cool slightly.
17. Sprinkle with confectioners' sugar.
Makes 24 pinwheels.
